title: alnus glutinosa lusus barbata
intro: Alnus glutinosa lusus barbata, also known as the Hairy Alder, is a variant of the black alder tree (Alnus glutinosa) native to Europe. The medicinal properties of the Hairy Alder include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with the Hairy Alder include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Hairy Alder is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
